Stub functions for reminder management in the Pebble firmware test suite.

# Purpose
This C++ header file provides a set of weakly linked functions related to managing reminders. The functions include [`reminders_update_timer`](<#reminders_update_timer>), [`reminders_init`](<#reminders_init>), [`reminders_handle_reminder_updated`](<#reminders_handle_reminder_updated>), [`reminders_can_snooze`](<#reminders_can_snooze>), and [`reminders_snooze`](<#reminders_snooze>). Each function is marked with the `WEAK` attribute, allowing them to be overridden by other implementations if needed. The functions return status codes or boolean values, with [`reminders_update_timer`](<#reminders_update_timer>), [`reminders_init`](<#reminders_init>), and [`reminders_snooze`](<#reminders_snooze>) returning a `status_t` type, while [`reminders_can_snooze`](<#reminders_can_snooze>) returns a boolean. The [`reminders_handle_reminder_updated`](<#reminders_handle_reminder_updated>) function takes a pointer to a `Uuid` as a parameter but does not return a value. The code imports dependencies from `system/status_codes.h` and `util/attributes.h`, indicating that it relies on external definitions for status codes and attribute handling.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `system/status_codes.h`
- `util/attributes.h`
